backlog.title: Orphaned-Run Lock Reclamation (FR-K1–K7, §9.27)
backlog.description: The v2.7 headline feature: when stagecoach's launcher closes without killing it (the lazygit/IDE case), a parent-death watchdog self-exits the run through the rescue + lock-release path so a reparented, still-running holder cannot hold the per-repo run lock forever. Adds SIGHUP to caught signals, a read-only lock status diagnostic, and a no_parent_watchdog opt-out. All other PRD features (v1–v2.6) are already implemented.

milestones.title: Signal Infrastructure — SIGHUP + Trigger Export
milestones.description: Extend the signal handler to catch SIGHUP (FR-K3) on Unix, and export a Trigger wrapper so the parent-death watchdog can programmatically invoke the rescue path (FR-K1 enabler). These are small additive changes to internal/signal — the existing handle()/run()/RestoreDefault are signal-agnostic and need no changes.

tasks.title: Add SIGHUP to caught signals (FR-K3)
tasks.description: The signal handler currently catches {SIGINT, SIGTERM} only. FR-K3 adds SIGHUP so a terminal hangup routes through the rescue path instead of Go's default disposition (terminate, skipping the deferred lock release and leaving the lock file). SIGHUP complements the parent-death watchdog: SIGHUP covers the terminal-hangup case where the signal IS delivered; the watchdog covers the detach/orphan case where it is NOT.

subtasks.title: Platform signal set helper + SIGHUP exit code
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/014_37208f58ffa2/architecture/signal_extension.md. The signal handler at internal/signal/signal.go:103 has `signal.Notify(h.ch, os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)`. This must be replaced with a build-tagged `caughtSignals()` helper. handle()/run()/RestoreDefault are signal-agnostic and need NO changes. RestoreDefault's `signal.Stop(h.ch)` disarms SIGHUP for free.
2. INPUT: The existing `signal.Notify` call at signal.go:103 and the `exitCodeForSignal` function in signal_unix.go:20-30 (currently maps SIGINT→130, SIGTERM→143, default→1).
3. LOGIC: (a) In internal/signal/signal_unix.go, add `func caughtSignals() []os.Signal { return []os.Signal{os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM, syscall.SIGHUP} }`. (b) In internal/signal/signal_windows.go, add `func caughtSignals() []os.Signal { return []os.Signal{os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM} }` — SIGHUP omitted on Windows (FR-K7). (c) In signal.go:103, replace `signal.Notify(h.ch, os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)` with `signal.Notify(h.ch, caughtSignals()...)`. (d) In signal_unix.go's `exitCodeForSignal`, add `case syscall.SIGHUP: return 129` (128+1). (e) Update the signal_unix.go comment on line 1 to mention SIGHUP. Mock nothing — this is direct signal package code with existing test patterns in signal_test.go.
4. OUTPUT: The signal handler now catches SIGHUP on Unix. A SIGHUP routes through handle() identically to SIGINT/SIGTERM: forward to child, cancel ctx, rescue-or-exit. Pre-snapshot SIGHUP exits 129; post-snapshot exits 3 (rescue). Windows unchanged.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the comment block at signal.go:1-2 to mention SIGHUP alongside SIGINT/SIGTERM. No external docs change for this subtask (the final docs task covers README/FAQ).
prd_selectors: h3.41 9.25 Hook execution on the commit path

tasks.title: Export signal.Trigger for programmatic rescue (FR-K1 enabler)
tasks.description: The parent-death watchdog detects parent death via getppid() polling but must programmatically invoke the rescue+exit path. handle() is unexported, so a new exported Trigger wrapper is needed. This is the single new signal-package API the watchdog requires.

subtasks.title: Add Trigger(sig) exported wrapper to signal package
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/014_37208f58ffa2/architecture/signal_extension.md. handle() is unexported (signal.go:122). The watchdog lives outside the signal package and cannot call it directly. signal.Active() is already exported (signal.go:110) and returns *Handler.
2. INPUT: The existing `active atomic.Pointer[Handler]` singleton (signal.go:72) and the unexported `handle(sig os.Signal)` method (signal.go:122-155) which does: check stopped → forward to child → cancel ctx → rescue-or-exit with OnRescueExit.
3. LOGIC: Add to internal/signal/signal.go a new exported function: `func Trigger(sig os.Signal) { if h := active.Load(); h != nil { h.handle(sig) } }`. This reuses handle() exactly — the stopped guard (first line of handle) prevents it from firing past RestoreDefault. The watchdog (P1.M2.T2.S1) will call `signal.Trigger(syscall.SIGTERM)` on parent death. Mock nothing — test via the existing installTestHandler pattern in signal_test.go (call Trigger and assert the Exit fake fired with the right code).
4. OUTPUT: `signal.Trigger(os.Signal)` — a nil-safe, stopped-guarded programmatic entry into the rescue path. No-op when no handler installed (library use) or after RestoreDefault (update-ref window). Consumed by the watchdog (P1.M2.T2.S1).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add a godoc comment on Trigger explaining its purpose (parent-death watchdog rescue trigger) and the stopped-guard guarantee. No external docs change.

milestones.title: Parent-Death Watchdog — Detection + Config + Arming (FR-K1/K2/K6/K7)
milestones.description: The core deliverable: a new internal/watchdog package that detects parent death (by parent-pid change, not getppid()==1) and routes the process through the existing rescue + lock-release exit path. Gated by a no_parent_watchdog config opt-out. Unix-only (Linux prctl fast path + getppid polling fallback; Darwin polling only). Windows is a no-op.

tasks.title: no_parent_watchdog config field (FR-K6)
tasks.description: Add the NoParentWatchdog config field via a 7-point copy from the existing NoVerify field pattern. Sources: env STAGECOACH_NO_PARENT_WATCHDOG, git config stagecoach.noParentWatchdog, TOML [generation].no_parent_watchdog. No CLI flag (unlike no_verify/no_color). Default off (watchdog runs by default).

subtasks.title: Add NoParentWatchdog config field with full 7-layer precedence
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/014_37208f58ffa2/architecture/watchdog_config.md. NoVerify is the exact 7-point copy template. The codebase uses ALL-CAPS env vars (STAGECOACH_*) and camelCase git config keys (stagecoach.noVerify). git rejects underscores in the final config segment per the codebase's own comment at git.go:180.
2. INPUT: The existing NoVerify field pattern across 7 files: config.go:142 (struct field `NoVerify bool toml:"no_verify"`), config.go:214 (Defaults `NoVerify: false`), file.go:68 (fileGeneration field), file.go:299 (materialize `if g.NoVerify { c.NoVerify = true }`), file.go:363 (overlay `if src.NoVerify { dst.NoVerify = true }`), load.go:320 (env `STAGECOACH_NO_VERIFY` DIRECT set), git.go:182 (git config `stagecoach.noVerify`).
3. LOGIC: Add `NoParentWatchdog` at each of the 7 touch points, mirroring NoVerify exactly: (a) config.go struct: `NoParentWatchdog bool \`toml:"no_parent_watchdog"\`` with doc comment referencing §9.27 FR-K6; (b) config.go Defaults: `NoParentWatchdog: false`; (c) file.go fileGeneration: `NoParentWatchdog bool \`toml:"no_parent_watchdog"\``; (d) file.go materialize: `if g.NoParentWatchdog { c.NoParentWatchdog = true }`; (e) file.go overlay: `if src.NoParentWatchdog { dst.NoParentWatchdog = true }`; (f) load.go loadEnv: `STAGECOACH_NO_PARENT_WATCHDOG` with strconv.ParseBool DIRECT set (can be false — escape hatch); (g) git.go loadGitConfig: `stagecoach.noParentWatchdog` via gitConfigBool. Do NOT add a CLI flag in root.go or an entry in loadFlags — FR-K6 has no flag. Also add a commented line to the bootstrap config template in internal/config/bootstrap.go (the config init generated template) documenting `# noParentWatchdog = false  # opt out of the parent-death lock watchdog (§9.27 FR-K6)`.
4. OUTPUT: cfg.NoParentWatchdog resolves through all 7 layers. Consumed by the watchdog arming in P1.M2.T2.S2 (`if !cfg.NoParentWatchdog { watchdog.Arm(...) }`).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the generated config template in bootstrap.go to document the new option. The bootstrap template's env-var reference block should add `#   STAGECOACH_NO_PARENT_WATCHDOG=1   # opt out of the parent-death lock watchdog (§9.27 FR-K6)`. The final docs task (P1.M4.T2) will cover README/docs for the overall feature.
prd_selectors: h3.77 16.1 Resolution order (FR34), lowest to highest

tasks.title: Watchdog implementation + arming (FR-K1/K2/K7)
tasks.description: Create the new internal/watchdog package that detects parent death and routes through signal.Trigger. Wire the arming in default_action.go after lock.Acquire, gated by cfg.NoParentWatchdog.

subtasks.title: Watchdog package — prctl fast path (Linux) + getppid polling (Unix), Windows no-op
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/014_37208f58ffa2/architecture/watchdog_config.md and external_deps.md. The watchdog must be a NEW package (internal/watchdog) — not in lock (which must stay stdlib-only without importing signal) or signal (which must stay stdlib-only without importing anything). The watchdog imports internal/signal (one-directional). prctl is per-thread (must LockOSThread); getppid()==1 is wrong under subreapers (use parent-pid CHANGE). See the researcher's findings on prctl race window, LockOSThread requirement, and the combined best-practice pattern.
2. INPUT: signal.Trigger(os.Signal) from P1.M1.T2.S1 — the programmatic rescue entry. The signal.Install context from main.go (the watchdog goroutine shares the signal-aware ctx so it dies when the process exits).
3. LOGIC: Create internal/watchdog/ with files: (a) watchdog.go — `func Arm(ctx context.Context, interval time.Duration)` records os.Getppid() at startup, starts the polling goroutine (ticks at interval, checks os.Getppid() != originalPpid, calls signal.Trigger(syscall.SIGTERM) on change). `func Stop()` cancels the watchdog (for tests/library use). On Linux, also call armPdeathsig best-effort. (b) arm_unix.go (`//go:build !windows`) — `func armImpl(originalPpid int, interval time.Duration, notify chan struct{})` starts getppid polling + calls armPdeathsig (best-effort on Linux via runtime.GOOS check). (c) arm_windows.go (`//go:build windows`) — `func armImpl(...)` is a no-op (FR-K7). (d) pdeathsig_linux.go (`//go:build linux`) — `func armPdeathsig(sig syscall.Signal) error` using `runtime.LockOSThread()` + `syscall.Syscall6(syscall.SYS_PRCTL, 1, uintptr(sig), 0,0,0,0)`. (e) pdeathsig_nonlinux.go (`//go:build !linux`) — `func armPdeathsig(sig syscall.Signal) error { return nil }`. The polling goroutine ALWAYS runs (even on Linux) as the fallback for the prctl race window and edge cases. The watchdog MUST be nil-safe: if signal.Active() returns nil (no handler installed), Trigger is a no-op and the goroutine exits cleanly on ctx cancel.
4. OUTPUT: `watchdog.Arm(ctx, interval)` — starts the parent-death watchdog goroutine. On parent death: calls signal.Trigger(SIGTERM) which routes through handle() → rescue if snapshot armed + OnRescueExit (lock release) + exit. The goroutine exits on ctx cancel (process exit). Consumed by P1.M2.T2.S2.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Each file needs a package/function godoc comment explaining its role and the FR-K requirement it implements. No external docs change for this subtask.
prd_selectors: h3.93 18.5 Concurrency: the per-repo run lock (FR52)

subtasks.title: Wire watchdog arming in default_action.go, gated by no_parent_watchdog
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/014_37208f58ffa2/architecture/watchdog_config.md. The lock is acquired in runDefault (default_action.go:71), NOT in main.go. The watchdog must arm AFTER lock.Acquire succeeds (the holder now owns the lock) and be GATED by cfg.NoParentWatchdog (FR-K6). The ctx passed to runDefault is the signal-aware ctx from main.go.
2. INPUT: `watchdog.Arm(ctx, interval)` from P1.M2.T2.S1, and `cfg.NoParentWatchdog` from P1.M2.T1.S1. The lock.Acquire site at default_action.go:71-78 (after `defer locker.Release()`).
3. LOGIC: In internal/cmd/default_action.go, immediately after the `defer locker.Release()` line (currently around line 78), add: `if !cfg.NoParentWatchdog { watchdog.Arm(ctx, 1*time.Second) }`. The 1-second interval is the default polling cadence (FR-K2). The watchdog goroutine dies with the process on exit (the ctx is canceled when main returns or the signal handler exits). Import `internal/watchdog` and `time` in default_action.go. Mock nothing — test via the e2e harness (P1.M4.T1.S1) which launches real stagecoach subprocesses.
4. OUTPUT: A stagecoach run that holds the lock now arms the parent-death watchdog unless opted out. When the launcher closes without killing the process, the watchdog detects the parent-pid change within ~1s and routes through signal.Trigger → rescue + lock release + exit.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add a comment at the arming site explaining the FR-K1/K6 gating. No external docs change.

milestones.title: Lock Diagnostics — lock status + Busy Message (FR-K4/K5)
milestones.description: Add a read-only `stagecoach lock status` subcommand (FR-K4) and reformat the Busy contention message with the lock path on its own line and an orphan hint (FR-K5). Both consume a shared orphan-detection helper.

tasks.title: Lock status read API + orphan detection (FR-K4)
tasks.description: Export a read-only Status() function from the lock package that returns the parsed lock-file state plus liveness and orphan detection. The orphan check reads the holder pid's parent (via /proc on Linux, ps on Darwin) and reports whether it appears reparented (parent is init/subreaper).

subtasks.title: Export Status() read path + orphan detection helper (Unix + Windows)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/014_37208f58ffa2/architecture/lock_extension.md. The lock package has unexported helpers: lockPath() (lock.go:302), parseContents() (lock.go:259), processAlive() (lock_unix.go:23). LockContents and HeldError are already exported. The orphan check is NEW platform code: read ANOTHER pid's parent (not our own getppid). Linux: /proc/<pid>/status PPid field. Darwin: `ps -o ppid= -p <pid>`. Windows: always false (FR-K7). The lock package must stay stdlib-only (os, os/exec, bufio, strconv are all stdlib).
2. INPUT: The existing lockPath(repoPath) function (returns the lock file path), parseContents(data) (returns LockContents), and processAlive(pid, hostname) (returns bool). The LockContents struct (Pid, Hostname, Repo, Timestamp, Snapshot string).
3. LOGIC: (a) Add `func Status(repoPath string) (path string, contents LockContents, alive bool, orphan bool, err error)` to internal/lock/lock.go. Implementation: call lockPath(repoPath) to get the path; os.ReadFile(path) — if file doesn't exist, return ("", LockContents{}, false, false, nil) meaning no lock; parseContents(data) to get contents; strconv.Atoi(contents.Pid) for the pid; processAlive(pid, contents.Hostname) for alive; if alive, appearsOrphaned(pid) for orphan. (b) Create internal/lock/orphan_unix.go (`//go:build !windows`): `func appearsOrphaned(pid int) bool` — on Linux read `/proc/<pid>/status`, parse `PPid:` line, return ppid==1; on Darwin (runtime.GOOS=="darwin"), shell out to `ps -o ppid= -p <pid>`, parse int, return ppid==1. On failure (file not found, ps error), return false (conservative — don't claim orphan on ambiguity). (c) Create internal/lock/orphan_windows.go (`//go:build windows`): `func appearsOrphaned(pid int) bool { return false }`. Mock nothing — test with temp lock files and known pids.
4. OUTPUT: `lock.Status(repoPath)` — a read-only diagnostic that returns the full lock state. path=="" means no lock held. Never acquires the flock, never breaks/removes a lock (FR52 preserved). Consumed by P1.M3.T2.S1 (lock status subcommand) and P1.M3.T3.S1 (Busy message orphan hint).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Godoc on Status() explaining it is read-only and never breaks a lock. Godoc on appearsOrphaned explaining the heuristic (ppid==1 ≈ reparented) and its limitations (subreapers may have ppid≠1).
prd_selectors: h3.93 18.5 Concurrency: the per-repo run lock (FR52)

tasks.title: stagecoach lock status subcommand (FR-K4)
tasks.description: Add a new `lock` command group with a `status` leaf that prints the lock holder's path, pid/hostname/repo/timestamp/snapshot, liveness, and orphan status. Read-only — never auto-breaks.

subtasks.title: Implement lock status cobra subcommand
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/014_37208f58ffa2/architecture/cli_test_extension.md. The hook.go command group is the best template: a cobra group cmd with a no-op PersistentPreRunE (overrides root's config.Load so it works outside a git repo and doesn't trigger first-run bootstrap), leaf subcommands, and init() registration on rootCmd with NO edit to root.go. Exit codes: Success=0, Error=1. lock status returns nil (exit 0) even when 'no lock held' — it's a read that found nothing.
2. INPUT: `lock.Status(repoPath)` from P1.M3.T1.S1. The hook.go pattern at internal/cmd/hook.go:38-58 (group cmd with no-op PersistentPreRunE) and hook.go:90-103 (init registration). The repoDir resolution via os.Getwd() (same as default_action.go:62).
3. LOGIC: Create internal/cmd/lock.go following the hook.go pattern: (a) `var lockCmd = &cobra.Command{Use: "lock", Short: "Inspect the per-repo run lock (FR52/§9.27)", SilenceErrors: true, SilenceUsage: true, PersistentPreRunE: func(*cobra.Command, []string) error { return nil }}` — the no-op PreRunE skips config.Load (diagnostic subcommand). (b) `var lockStatusCmd = &cobra.Command{Use: "status", Short: "Print the run lock holder's pid/host/repo/liveness/orphan-status", Args: cobra.NoArgs, SilenceErrors: true, SilenceUsage: true, RunE: runLockStatus}`. (c) `func init() { lockCmd.AddCommand(lockStatusCmd); rootCmd.AddCommand(lockCmd) }`. (d) `func runLockStatus(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error` — resolve repoDir via os.Getwd(); call lock.Status(repoDir); print to cmd.OutOrStdout(). Format: 'Lock: <path>' + indented fields (pid, hostname, repo, timestamp, snapshot if set, alive, orphaned). If path is empty: 'no run lock for <repoDir>'. Return exitcode.New(exitcode.Error, ...) on error, nil on success.
4. OUTPUT: `stagecoach lock status` — a read-only diagnostic. Never auto-breaks (FR52 preserved). The user learns the lock path and the holder's liveness/orphan status, then decides whether to kill/rm themselves.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] godoc on runLockStatus explaining the read-only contract. The final docs task (P1.M4.T2) will add this to the README's CLI reference.

tasks.title: Busy message reformat — lock path on own line + orphan hint (FR-K5)
tasks.description: Reformat the contention Busy message so the lock path is on its own line (scriptable), and add an orphan hint when the holder appears orphaned.

subtasks.title: Refactor handleLockContention with orphan hint and lock path on own line
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/014_37208f58ffa2/architecture/lock_extension.md. The current Busy message at default_action.go:314-322 buries 'Lock: %s.' mid-sentence. FR-K5 puts it on its own line. When the holder appears orphaned (reuses the orphan detection from P1.M3.T1.S1), add a hint that the user may safely kill/rm. The existing fallback diagnostics (lines 298-312: '<unknown>' for empty pid/hostname, 'an unknown repo' for empty repo) and the no-op fast path (lines 287-295: snapshot match → exit 0) must be preserved unchanged.
2. INPUT: The existing handleLockContention function at default_action.go:285-323. The heldErr.Contents (LockContents with Pid, Hostname) and heldErr.Path. The lock.appearsOrphaned helper from P1.M3.T1.S1 (exported or accessible via lock.Status). The lock.processAlive helper (for checking liveness before orphan check).
3. LOGIC: Modify handleLockContention in internal/cmd/default_action.go: (a) Keep the no-op fast path (snapshot match → exit 0) unchanged. (b) Keep the fallback diagnostics (repo/pid/hostname substitution) unchanged. (c) Change the Fprintf format: put the main message first (without the trailing 'Lock: %s.'), then a blank line, then 'Lock: <path>' on its own line. (d) After the lock path line, if the holder pid is non-empty and appears orphaned (call lock.Status or lock.appearsOrphaned with the parsed pid), add a line: 'The holder's launcher appears to have exited — it may be orphaned and holding this lock uselessly. You may safely `kill <pid>` or `rm <path>` to clear it. See `stagecoach lock status`.' (e) Update lock_contention_test.go assertions to match the new format. NOTE: need to export appearsOrphaned or add a helper that takes LockContents and returns orphan status (the cmd package can call lock.Status or a new lock.IsOrphaned(pid) export).
4. OUTPUT: The Busy message now has the lock path on its own line and flags orphaned holders. The no-op fast path and fallback diagnostics are unchanged. Tests in lock_contention_test.go are updated.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] No external docs change for this subtask — the final docs task covers the README.
prd_selectors: h3.93 18.5 Concurrency: the per-repo run lock (FR52)

milestones.title: E2E Tests + Documentation Sync
milestones.description: Add e2e scenario tests for the orphaned-run lock reclamation (§20.5) and sync changeset-level documentation (README.md, docs/).

tasks.title: E2E scenario tests for orphaned-run lock reclamation (§20.5)
tasks.description: Per §20.5: launch stagecoach from a short-lived parent that exits mid-generation and assert the holder self-exits via the parent-death watchdog, the lock file is removed, and HEAD+index are unchanged. Drive SIGHUP. Assert lock status reports correctly. Assert the opt-out suppresses the watchdog.

subtasks.title: Orphaned-run lock reclamation e2e scenarios
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/014_37208f58ffa2/architecture/cli_test_extension.md. The existing e2e harness lives in internal/e2e/ with `//go:build e2e` tag. lock_scenarios_test.go already tests contention with REAL stagecoach subprocesses. The harness uses buildStagecoach/newRepo/runStagecoach/waitForMarker/writeStubConfig/stubEnv. The stub agent supports STAGECOACH_STUB_SLEEP_MS for blocking mid-generation. The getppid polling interval is 1s — tests must allow ~2-3s for detection + exit.
2. INPUT: The harness primitives from internal/e2e/harness_test.go. The stub agent from cmd/stubagent/main.go (supports STAGECOACH_STUB_MARKER + STAGECOACH_STUB_SLEEP_MS). All implemented features from P1.M1–P1.M3.
3. LOGIC: Add scenarios to internal/e2e/lock_scenarios_test.go (or a new file): (a) Parent-death watchdog: spawn stagecoach from a parent process (e.g. `sh -c 'exec stagecoach ...'` whose shell exits after exec), with the stub agent sleeping 3s. The parent's shell exits immediately (stagecoach is reparented). Assert: within ~3s the stagecoach process exits (watchdog detected parent death), the lock file is removed (check the lock dir), HEAD is unchanged (no commit landed), index is unchanged. (b) SIGHUP rescue: start stagecoach with the stub sleeping, send SIGHUP to the stagecoach pid via syscall.Kill. Assert: rescue path fires (exit 3 if snapshot armed, or the process exits), lock file removed. (c) lock status diagnostics: write a lock file with known pid/hostname/repo, run `stagecoach lock status` via the CLI, assert the output contains the expected fields. Test with a live pid (alive=true), a dead pid (alive=false), and check orphan status. With no lock, assert 'no run lock'. (d) Opt-out: set STAGECOACH_NO_PARENT_WATCHDOG=1 (or stagecoach.noParentWatchdog=true via git config), repeat the parent-death scenario, assert the watchdog does NOT fire (process keeps running until the stub finishes). NOTE: the parent-death test is timing-sensitive (1s polling + process exit); use generous timeouts. The SIGHUP test can send the signal directly.
4. OUTPUT: E2e test coverage for FR-K1/K3/K4/K6. Every bug found in the wild becomes a scenario here. These tests run with `go test -tags e2e` and require the built stagecoach binary.
5. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change. Test code only.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Update README.md, docs/, and any overview documentation to surface the new lock status subcommand, the SIGHUP/watchdog behavior, and the no_parent_watchdog opt-out. This is the Mode B catch-all for cross-cutting doc changes.

subtasks.title: Update README.md, docs/ for orphaned-run lock reclamation
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The README at README.md already has a features section, CLI reference, and FAQ. The docs/ directory has providers.md and other reference docs. The PRD §15.3 lists `stagecoach lock status` as a new subcommand. The PRD §16.3 shows `noParentWatchdog = false` in the git config example.
2. INPUT: All implementing subtasks from P1.M1–P1.M3. The README.md current content. The docs/ directory structure.
3. LOGIC: (a) README.md: add `stagecoach lock status` to the CLI reference / subcommands list. Add a brief mention of the parent-death watchdog in the concurrency/safety section (the lock page or the FAQ). Add `no_parent_watchdog` to the config/env vars reference. (b) docs/: if there is a lock.md or concurrency reference, update it with the orphaned-run reclamation behavior. (c) Ensure the FAQ entry about the run lock mentions that orphaned holders self-exit (the lazygit/IDE case). (d) Run a grep for any stale references to SIGINT/SIGTERM-only signal handling and update to include SIGHUP. Ensure no docs claim 'SIGINT/SIGTERM only'.
4. OUTPUT: README.md and docs/ are consistent with the implemented FR-K1–K7 feature set. A user reading the docs can discover the lock status subcommand, the watchdog behavior, and the opt-out.
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This IS the documentation sync task. Update README.md features list, CLI reference, and FAQ. Update docs/ reference files.
